skill.name: ui-style-guide
skill.description: Frontend coding standards, component patterns, and design system guidance for the Guessimate Angular UI.
skill.summary: Apply consistent Angular, Tailwind, and design system conventions for the Guessimate frontend.
skill.author: DavidOpDeBeeck

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review shows these are Markdown fences, inline code examples, headings, color tokens, and prose, not executable logic. No network, filesystem, environment, prompt injection, or command execution intent was found in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent frontend code slows review and weakens the product interface. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear Angular and Tailwind standards for Guessimate UI work.
actual_capabilities: Defines the Guessimate Angular 20 frontend technology stack.
Explains the feature-based application directory structure.
Documents naming rules for files, classes, selectors, signals, and observables.
Guides standalone components, inline templates, Tailwind utility classes, and Angular control flow.
Describes state patterns using signals, computed values, stores, and limited effects.
Lists semantic color, dark mode, card, container, and typography conventions.
limitations: Focused on the Guessimate Angular frontend, not general web projects.
Provides guidance only and does not run linting or code generation.
Does not include a complete reusable component library.
Assumes familiarity with Angular, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript.

use_cases.title: Build a New Feature Screen
use_cases.description: Use the guide to place files, name components, and match Guessimate UI patterns.

use_cases.title: Review Angular Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Check component structure, state handling, Tailwind usage, and visual consistency before merge.

use_cases.title: Align Design System Decisions
use_cases.description: Reference colors, dark mode behavior, cards, containers, and typography during interface planning.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Style Fit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small frontend change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Angular UI change against the Guessimate style guide. Focus on naming, file placement, Tailwind classes, and dark mode.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a new Angular component before coding.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Guessimate Angular component for this feature. Include folder location, component responsibilities, state approach, and styling rules.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing UI
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving a component that does not match local conventions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Angular component to follow the Guessimate guide. Keep behavior unchanged and explain each design system adjustment.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Feature Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a larger feature area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this feature module for Guessimate frontend standards. Assess structure, signals, stores, dependency injection, templates, and visual patterns.

output_examples.input: Review a session page component for guide compliance.
output: The component belongs under the session feature area.
Signal names are clear and do not need suffixes.
The template should use semantic color tokens for dark mode consistency.

output_examples.input: Plan styling for a new estimation card.
output: Use the standard surface, border, radius, and divider conventions.
Keep spacing on theme values instead of arbitrary pixel values.
Add dark mode classes for every color-related choice.

output_examples.input: Choose state management for shared lobby data.
output: Use a feature store when multiple session components share the data.
Expose derived values with computed signals.
Reserve effects for necessary side effects only.

best_practices: Use feature folders as the default home for domain UI code.
Prefer signals, computed values, and explicit imports for Angular components.
Apply semantic colors and dark mode classes together.
anti_patterns: Do not organize new feature code only by technical type.
Do not add arbitrary Tailwind values when theme values work.
Do not use effects for ordinary derived state.

faq.question: What projects is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the Guessimate Angular frontend and its local conventions.

faq.question: Can it generate complete components?
faq.answer: It can guide component structure and style, but it is not a component generator by itself.

faq.question: Does it support Angular standalone components?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide recommends standalone components and explicit imports.

faq.question: Does it include Tailwind guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Tailwind CSS 4, semantic colors, theme values, and dark mode classes.

faq.question: Can reviewers use it during pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives concrete checks for naming, structure, state, templates, and visual patterns.

faq.question: Does it perform security scanning?
faq.answer: No. It is a frontend style guide and does not execute scans or commands.
